purpose: Treat SiteSift as one product across email-admin-ui, EmailAutomation, Firestore, Microsoft Graph, Google Sheets, and operator evidence before any production widening.

requiredBeforeProductionWidening: identify changed feature ids in feature-registry.json
walk every send-risk feature through this matrix
run the fixture classes listed for each touched feature
request CodeRabbit review using this packet and both repo AGENTS.md files
read back Firestore, Graph/Sent Items, Sheet, dashboard, and actionAudit evidence
keep normal users paused or scoped off until the base proof passes

crossRepoInvariants.rule: Normal users cannot trigger Tour Scheduling email, drafts, pending invites, tour reply handling, or tour-specific dashboard actions from Production V1.
Raw placeholders such as [NAME], {{name}}, local-part greetings, and blank first names must be blocked before outbox creation and again before Graph send.
Every queued, sent, skipped, duplicate-skipped, cancelled, failed, or dead-lettered send must be visible in actionAudit or an operator recovery state.
crossRepoInvariants.incidentClass: hidden failed send or unresolved action loss

crossRepoInvariants.rule: Any retry or recovery send must check recent Sent Items, thread state, and manual user continuation before sending again.
crossRepoInvariants.incidentClass: manual user reply followed by autonomous duplicate

crossRepoInvariants.rule: Frontend feature hiding is never enough; backend-capable Results, Tour, Usage, scheduler, and recovery behavior must fail closed by entitlement and user scope.
Dashboard replies, auto-replies, follow-ups, and recovery sends must preserve safe reply-all Cc context while filtering blocked or unrelated recipients.

emailBehavior: Queues initial campaign outreach only after column/name/property review passes.
Must not queue raw placeholders or tour-scheduling copy.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can this upload path queue email with a placeholder, wrong recipient, or normal-user tour language?

emailBehavior: Resolves the greeting/name variable before any draft or send.
Blocks [NAME], blank names, local-part greetings, and ambiguous contact columns.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can any recipient/name variation let a placeholder or wrong first name reach outbox or send?

emailBehavior: Creates initial outreach drafts for approved campaign rows.
Must not introduce tour, LOI, client-visit, or unresolved-variable language into Production V1.
codeRabbitQuestions: Does this launch draft path send or queue unsafe copy, bad placeholders, tour language, or missing audit evidence?

emailBehavior: Autonomously sends already-approved outbox records through Microsoft Graph.
Must dedupe partial successes, honor cancel-after-claim, and record Graph message identity when available.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can this autonomous send duplicate, lose audit, hide a failure, send to a wrong recipient, or miss Sent Items reconciliation?

emailBehavior: Preserves safe broker Cc/reply-all context for dashboard and automated replies while filtering blocked contacts.
codeRabbitQuestions: Does this reply-all path preserve safe CC recipients, filter bad recipients, and audit who received the send?

emailBehavior: Queues or sends operator-approved replies only after exact body, recipient, signature, and action state are visible.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can this manual reply hide failure, clear the action early, drop CCs, or send placeholders/wrong signatures?

emailBehavior: May draft or queue core information-gathering responses only under Production V1 rules.
Must not auto-schedule tours or respond after terminal/manual continuation state.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can inbox processing send an autonomous reply with tour language, bad placeholders, duplicate retry, or hidden failure?

emailBehavior: Autonomously sends follow-ups only after checking inbound replies, manual user replies, opt-outs, stopped/completed/max states, and recent Sent Items.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can this follow-up send after a broker reply, manual user reply, opt-out, terminal state, or duplicate-retry condition?

emailBehavior: Uses the signed-in user's selected signature and organization-safe defaults for every send path.
Must not leak Jill/MOHR identity to unrelated users.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can this signature path send with missing identity, wrong organization, raw placeholder, or Jill/MOHR leakage?

emailBehavior: Recovery sends are explicit operator actions only, never hidden automatic retries.
Must recheck Sent Items/manual continuation before send.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can recovery hide a failure, delete evidence, duplicate-send, or send without a visible audit trail?

emailBehavior: Controls which users can be processed by autonomous worker runs.
Must stay explicitly scoped during recovery and never surprise-process paused users.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can this scheduler change widen normal user scope, trigger unexpected sends, or hide failed queue state?

emailBehavior: Dev-only tour invite queueing; never normal-user Production V1.
Requires explicit allowlist and preview approval before any email side effect.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can a normal user trigger tour invite email, queued tour outbox, or hidden tour audit from Results?

emailBehavior: Dev-only tour reply classification and response drafting.
Must distinguish tour unavailable from property unavailable and never run for normal users.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can tour reply handling misclassify unavailable tours, send for a normal user, or create duplicate replies?

emailBehavior: Dev-only alternate-time response drafting tied to itinerary conflicts.
Must not generic-reply or alter normal broker outreach.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can alternate-time tour code send without entitlement, miss a conflict, duplicate-send, or hide operator failure?

emailBehavior: Future Firebase-native scheduler must preserve the same user scope, order, lease, and send guards as the GitHub worker.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can the cloud scheduler widen user scope, change send order, bypass audit, or process normal users unexpectedly?

frontendSurfaces: operator recovery/status surfaces if added later
emailBehavior: Future task worker must preserve actionAudit, dedupe, recipient validation, Sent Items/manual-continuation checks, and dead-letter visibility.
codeRabbitQuestions: Can Cloud Tasks create duplicate sends, hide failure, bypass recipient checks, or skip audit terminalization?
